---
title: MGNify
alias: &ALIAS mgnify
href: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/metagenomics/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

MGNify (EMBL-EBI) is a free-to-use platform for assembly, analysis, and archiving of microbiome-derived sequence data, supporting metagenomic and metatranscriptomic research.

## Key Features

- Processes and archives amplicon, metagenomic, and metatranscriptomic sequencing datasets
- Provides standardized taxonomic profiling, functional annotation, and assembly pipelines
- Hosts a large catalog of analyzed studies with interactive summary statistics
- Offers programmatic REST API access to processed results and assembled sequences
- Supports biome classification across gut, soil, ocean, and other environmental contexts

## Use Cases in Research

- Accessing uniformly processed gut microbiome datasets for comparative AMR gene analysis
- Mining metagenome-assembled genomes (MAGs) for novel resistance gene discovery
- Performing taxonomic and functional profiling comparisons across microbiome studies
- Integrating MGNify outputs with host transcriptomic data for microbiome-host interaction studies
- Using standardized pipelines to validate in-house metagenomics workflows

## Getting Started

Visit [MGNify](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/metagenomics/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
